mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously as long as the rate trajectory is downward.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 2.41% suggests room for further easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk and equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 3.8% is below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. This interaction effect predicts negative 20-day returns as equity investors anticipate lower corporate earnings from reduced consumer spending.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 505969M and HOUST at 1162K require trend analysis for contraction signal
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces negative 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) when tested against single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) above 111.0 indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power, supporting corporate profit margins and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors recognize earnings resilience.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 110.475 is near but below trigger threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns when INDPRO > 111.0 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 50,000 new jobs per month signals labor market softening and reduced consumer income growth, triggering negative equity returns over 60 days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: month-over-month PAYEMS growth < 50,000 for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:current PAYEMS at 150816K requires month-over-month change calculation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 50K for 2 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 3.0%, the real policy rate becomes negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 254.148 and FEDFUNDS at 2.41% require YoY calculation
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) when tested against additive single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 1.5% annualized signals late-cycle economic weakness and reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprice terminal growth rates.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 1.5% for 2 consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 18765.256B requires quarterly growth calculation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% for 2 quarters, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) above 1,300K combined with retail sales (RSAFS) growth above 0.5% month-over-month indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, supporting corporate earnings and producing positive 5-day returns as forward guidance improves.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1162K is below threshold, RSAFS at 505969M requires MoM calculation
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with strong consumer activity
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 5-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) when tested against single-series models.
